misconceptionSummary: Students memorise names of special sequences without linking them to their defining growth patterns.

visuals.ruleText: Special sequences are recognised by their defining pattern
visuals.subText: The name matches the growth rule

steps.narration: Special sequences are identified by how they are built.

lines.text: Fibonacci: add the previous two terms

steps.narration: Fibonacci is defined by combining the last two terms.
Square and cube sequences come from powers of counting numbers.
Triangular numbers build by adding the next counting number each time.

visuals.expression: Recognise the defining rule before continuing the sequence
visuals.label: Name and pattern belong together

steps.narration: The conceptual key is matching each sequence name to its growth structure.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: memorising examples without understanding the rule behind them. The rule is what lets you recognise new examples.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If you know the growth rule, you can recognise the sequence even when the numbers are unfamiliar.
